channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: As Global Conflicts Go Digital, Businesses Need Wartime Gameplans
item.description: The fate of a Ukrainian tax software company shows how modern cyberwarfare can claim casualties far beyond the battlefield, and how businesses across the ocean still need to protect themselves.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: UK Government Rolls Out Agentic AI Defense Plan Alongside Industry Pledge
item.description: <p>Two announcements on July 7, 2026, demonstrate the government’s determination to improve the level of cybersecurity within the UK.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/uk-government-rolls-out-agentic-ai-defense-plan-alongside-industry-pledge/">UK Government Rolls Out Agentic AI Defense Plan Alongside Industry Pledge</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: Vibe-Coded Malware Caught in Active Directory Attack
item.description: Huntress found a threat actor using vibe-coded PowerShell to map an Active Directory network

item.title: Palo Alto Networks Patches 13 Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>Buffer overflow, DoS, command injection, SSRF, authentication bypass, and other types of vulnerabilities have been found in PAN-OS software.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/palo-alto-networks-patches-13-vulnerabilities/">Palo Alto Networks Patches 13 Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
item.description: A cryptomining incident highlights how AI gateways can provide access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and identity and access management (IAM) data.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: AI Attacks Move in Minutes. Join This Webinar on Building a Defense That Keeps Up
item.description:
AI has changed how fast attacks move. Work that once took an attacker days now takes minutes. Using models like Mythos, attackers write tailored bait, pick targets, test what lands, and jump to the next host before your team clears the first alert.
That is the gap, and it is not your fault. The tools and runbooks most teams run on were built for attackers who work at human speed. AI-driven
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 12 Million Impacted by Data Breach at Japanese Telco KDDI
item.description: <p>Hackers exploited a zero-day vulnerability in a third-party system to access a KDDI email system for ISPs.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/12-million-impacted-by-data-breach-at-japanese-telco-kddi/">12 Million Impacted by Data Breach at Japanese Telco KDDI</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: 75% CISOs Fear Executives Don’t Understand Cybersecurity Risks Employees Face
item.description: Survey of cybersecurity leaders by MetaCompliance finds that many feel boards are uninterested in ever-evolving cyber risks

item.title: 15-Year-Old Linux Vulnerability ‘GhostLock’ Earns Researchers $92k From Google
item.description: <p>Affecting every major distribution since 2011, the Linux kernel vulnerability allows attackers to gain root access.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/15-year-old-linux-vulnerability-ghostlock-earns-researchers-92k-from-google/">15-Year-Old Linux Vulnerability &#8216;GhostLock&#8217; Earns Researchers $92k From Google</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Chinese-Funded Interpol Cybercrime Crackdown Leads to 5,800 Arrests
item.description: Operation First Light 2026, coordinated by Interpol and funded by the Chinese government, has led to 5,811 arrests

item.title: The Language of AI Could Change How Humans Speak
item.description: <p>Because of the way they are trained, large language models capture only a slice of human language. They&#8217;re trained on the written word, from textbooks to social media posts, and our speech as captured in movies and on television. These models have minimal access to the unscripted conversations we have face to face or voice to voice. This is the vast majority of speech, and a vital component of human culture.</p> <p>There&#8217;s a risk to this. The increased use of large language models means we humans will encounter much more AI-generated text. We humans, in turn, will begin to adopt the linguistic patterns and behaviors of these models. This will affect not just how we communicate with one another, but also how we ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Summer of Clearinghouses
item.description: Everyone seems to have announced a clearinghouse over the past few weeks. We did too. Ours is called Athena, and the main thing that sets it apart is that it was already real and running when we announced it — built quietly months earlier, heads down, taking findings and shipping fixes, because customers kept asking us to. We only announced it now because everyone else started announcing theirs,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GhostApproval Flaw Hits Six Major AI Coding Assistants
item.description: Wiz discovered GhostApproval, a symlink flaw in six major AI coding assistants that bypasses approval

item.title: GodDamn Ransomware Uses PoisonX Driver to Disable Endpoint Defenses
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new ransomware family called GodDamn that employs the PoisonX kernel driver to neutralize security software as part of its defense evasion strategy.
According to a new report published by the Threat Hunter Team from Symantec, the ransomware was first publicly spotted in the wild on May 21, 2026. It's assessed to be a rebrand of the Beast ransomware,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Patches Defender ‘RoguePlanet’ Vulnerability
item.description: <p>The privilege escalation vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-50656 has been patched with a Microsoft Malware Protection Engine update.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/microsoft-patches-defender-rogueplanet-vulnerability/">Microsoft Patches Defender &#8216;RoguePlanet&#8217; Vulnerability</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Mount Royal University Confirms Data Stolen in Ransomware Attack
item.description: <p>Hackers accessed the institution’s internal network and deleted two drives containing employee, student, and university data.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/mount-royal-university-confirms-data-stolen-in-ransomware-attack/">Mount Royal University Confirms Data Stolen in Ransomware Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: 'GodDamn' Ransomware Uses BYOVD to Smite US Companies
item.description: Microsoft signed a malicious kernel driver, and now it's being used to kill security software in ransomware attacks.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: New AI Security Charter Backed by Over 70 Cyber Firms
item.description: Over 70 cybersecurity organizations have signed the CREST AI Charter detailing responsible use of AI for security

item.title: AI Coding Tools Tricked Into Hacking Developer Machine via Decades-Old Technique
item.description: <p>Wiz has disclosed the details of a new AI coding assistant attack method it has dubbed GhostApproval.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/ai-coding-tools-tricked-into-hacking-developer-machine-via-decades-old-technique/">AI Coding Tools Tricked Into Hacking Developer Machine via Decades-Old Technique</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Microsoft Patches RoguePlanet Defender Flaw That Can Grant SYSTEM Privileges
item.description:
Microsoft has released security updates for a Defender vulnerability known as RoguePlanet, nearly a month after details of the flaw became public.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), is a privilege escalation issue in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine ("mpengine.dll"), which provides scanning, detection, and cleaning capabilities for its antivirus and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: European Organizations Have a Collaboration Security Confidence Gap
item.description: A new survey shows security leaders have an inflated sense of safety regarding their collaboration tools and platforms.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Cybercriminals Plant Malicious AI Agents in Open Source Tool Repositories
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers at ESET identify big rise in suspicious and malicious toolsets which put users at risk from cyber-attacks

item.title: Chrome 150 Update Patches 27 Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>The security refresh resolves 13 use-after-free bugs, including two critical-severity flaws found by Google.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/chrome-150-update-patches-27-vulnerabilities/">Chrome 150 Update Patches 27 Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Meta's New AI Image Tool Lets Others Use Your Public Instagram Photos in AI Images
item.description:
Meta has announced that its new artificial intelligence (AI) model Muse Image lets people use public Instagram posts and reels to generate AI content, and it's enabled by default.
"You can also @-mention Instagram accounts in the Meta AI app to bring specific Instagram profiles right into your images," the social media giant said in a post.
"Whether you want to design a custom event invitation
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 8Layers Raises $2.9 Million for Identity Security Platform
item.description: <p>The Spanish startup has closed an extended pre-seed funding round two months after launching its digital identity protection platform.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/8layers-raises-2-9-million-for-identity-security-platform/">8Layers Raises $2.9 Million for Identity Security Platform</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
item.description:
Ask an AI coding agent to scan open-source code for security holes, and it might run the attacker's code on your own machine instead.
That is the finding in a&nbsp;proof-of-concept published Wednesday&nbsp;by the AI Now Institute, an attack it calls "Friendly Fire." It works against Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex when either is running in an autonomous mode that approves its own
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Unpatched Backdoor in Tenda Firmware Grants Admin Access to Devices
item.description: <p>Tracked as CVE-2026-11405, the vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to access a device's web management interface.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/unpatched-backdoor-in-tenda-firmware-grants-admin-access-to-devices/">Unpatched Backdoor in Tenda Firmware Grants Admin Access to Devices</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: GhostApproval Symlink Flaws Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code in AI Coding Agents
item.description:
Researchers at&nbsp;Wiz&nbsp;found that a flaw in six popular AI coding assistants lets a booby-trapped code project quietly take control of a developer's computer. The assistant asks permission to edit one harmless-looking file, but the write lands on a sensitive one instead.
The affected tools are Amazon Q Developer, Anthropic's Claude Code, Augment, Cursor, Google Antigravity, and Windsurf.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake 7-Zip Installers Turn Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new threat actor dubbed Lurking Lizard that has been operating an end-to-end malicious residential proxy business using an infrastructure comprising more than 230 lookalike domains.
The activity dates back to at least August 2022, according to DNS threat intelligence firm Infoblox. Once such campaign, observed earlier this year, involved the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Best Cyber Threat Detection Tools & Software | UpGuard
item.description: The best cyber threat detection tools and software in 2026. Learn how to protect your endpoints, logs, and external digital surfaces from a breach.

item.title: Mexico's New Cyber Plan Faces Its First Real Test
item.description: The Latin American nation's cybersecurity plan — still in the expansion phase — has to survive its own knockout round during the FIFA World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
item.description: The attacker exploited AI workflows, chained cloud weaknesses, and stolen credentials to extort a large Amazon customer.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Windows 11 Cleanup Guide: 9 Ways to Make Your PC Feel Faster
item.description: <p>Make Windows 11 feel faster with nine cleanup tips for startup apps, storage, background processes, updates, malware scans, and more.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-windows-11-cleanup-guide/">Windows 11 Cleanup Guide: 9 Ways to Make Your PC Feel Faster</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Matt Gonzales

item.title: AI Coding Agents Found Triggering Endpoint Security Rules Built to Catch Attackers
item.description:
Sophos looked at a week of its own endpoint data and found that AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex are setting off detection rules written to catch human intruders.
The agents are not malicious. They just do a lot of things that, to a behavioral engine, look exactly like an attack.
Decrypting browser credentials, listing what sits in Windows' credential store,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake Job Offers Impersonate Netflix, OpenAI, and FIFA to Steal Google Credentials
item.description: <p>A fake recruitment phishing campaign impersonates major brands and uses trusted HR platforms to steal Google account credentials.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fake-recruitment-phishing-google-credentials-2026/">Fake Job Offers Impersonate Netflix, OpenAI, and FIFA to Steal Google Credentials</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Vidar Infostealer Hammers SMBs via Malvertising Campaign
item.description: A financially motivated operation uses lures of cracked or pirated software to deliver a two-for-one malware combo for data theft and cryptomining.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Australia’s Teen Social Media Ban Exposes Age Verification Gap
item.description: <p>Australia’s under-16 social media ban faces scrutiny after testers found major platforms did not ask for age proof.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-australia-teen-social-media-ban-age-checks-apac/">Australia’s Teen Social Media Ban Exposes Age Verification Gap</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Hacker Claims Accenture Breach Exposed Source Code, SSH Keys, and Azure Tokens
item.description: <p>Accenture confirmed a breach after a hacker claimed 35GB of source code and cloud keys were stolen, raising questions for cloud and security teams.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-accenture-breach-cloud-keys/">Hacker Claims Accenture Breach Exposed Source Code, SSH Keys, and Azure Tokens</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: RedWing Android Spyware Sold as a Service on Telegram
item.description: Zimperium found RedWing, an Android spyware sold as a service via Telegram to target banking apps

item.title: New HalluSquatting Attack Could Trick AI Coding Assistants Into Installing Botnet Malware
item.description:
AI coding assistants have a habit of making things up. Ask one to fetch a popular tool, and it will sometimes hand back a real-sounding name for a project that does not exist.
New research, which its authors call&nbsp;HalluSquatting, turns that habit into an attack: work out the fake names an AI reliably invents, register them first, and wait for the assistant to fetch your trap on a user's
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ubiquiti Patches Critical UniFi Flaws Across Connect, Talk, Access, Protect, and OS
item.description:
Ubiquiti has shipped updates to address multiple critical security flaws impacting UniFi Connect, UniFi Talk, UniFi Access, UniFi Protect, and UniFi OS that could result in privilege escalation and arbitrary command execution.
The list of vulnerabilities is as follows -
CVE-2026-50746 (CVSS score: 10.0) - An improper access control vulnerability in UniFi Connect Application that an attacker
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China-Linked APT Expands Proxy Network With New Malware
item.description: Cisco Talos said China-linked APT UAT-7810 is growing its proxy relay network with new malware

item.title: New Ghost Phishing Wave Is Breaking Traditional Email Security
item.description:
A recent EvilTokens campaign targeting businesses across the US and Europe is exposing a new email security blind spot. This “ghost phishing” technique keeps the malicious page hidden until it decrypts and comes to life inside the victim’s browser.
For security leaders, the risk is clear: traditional URL checks may miss the attack while Microsoft 365 access, sensitive data, and response time
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SCMBANKER Malware Uses ClickFix Lures to Target Mexican Banking Users
item.description:
A new banking fraudulent operation is targeting customers of Mexican banks, fintech, payment processors, and cryptocurrency exchanges using ClickFix lures.
The activity cluster, tracked by Elastic Security Labs under the moniker REF6045, involves infecting victims through fake CAPTCHA verification pages that deceive them into running a malicious command that installs a PowerShell toolkit dubbed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
item.description: A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Threat Actors Uses Agentic AI to Rapidly Compromise Cloud Target
item.description: Sygnia report details how agentic AI accelerated weeks-long attack to just 72 hours

item.title: GitHub 'Verified' Commits Can Be Rewritten Into New Hashes Without Breaking Signatures
item.description:
New research shows that a signed Git commit's hash is not the one-of-a-kind name that much of the software world assumes it to be. Given any signed commit, someone without the signing key can mint a second commit with the same files, author, and date, and a valid signature, GitHub still stamps "Verified."
Everything a reviewer would check matches. The commit's hash does not. That matters
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Verification Step Is the New ATO Battleground in 2026
item.description:
For years, account takeover (ATO) followed a predictable script. Attackers bought stolen credentials in bulk, ran them through automated tools, and waited for matches. Credential stuffing was cheap, scalable, and for defenders, relatively well understood.
That era is ending. Not because attackers gave up, but because the front door finally got harder to kick in.
Passkeys are now mainstream.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GitHub Copilot Refuses Harmful Requests in Chat, Then Writes Them in Code
item.description:
An AI coding assistant that refuses to answer a dangerous request in its chat box can answer it anyway if the same request is broken into small, ordinary-looking steps inside a code editor. That is the finding of a&nbsp;new study of GitHub Copilot&nbsp;by researchers Abhishek Kumar and Carsten Maple.
The models they tested through Copilot, Claude from Anthropic, and Gemini from Google, refused
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cybersecurity and the Gap Between Skill and Ability
item.description: <p>Last week, national security agencies from the Five Eyes&#8212;that&#8217;s the rich, English-language-speaking countries club&#8212;jointly released a <a href="https://www.nsa.gov/Press-Room/News-Highlights/Article/Article/4523810/five-eyes-cyber-security-agencies-statement/">statement</a> warning of the increasing cyber risks of AI models: in particular, their ability to autonomously hack into systems and networks. The statement was more measured than some of the <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jun/22/anthropic-claude-fable-ai-model-artificial-intelligence-national-security">breathless headlines</a> about it, and the advice they gave is pretty much the standard advice everyone gives&#8212;albeit with newfound urgency.</p> <p>Internet risks are nothing new, and cyberattacks&#8212;both large and small&#8212;have been a significant issue since long before the current crop of generative AI models...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: New Malicious Campaign Delivers Vidar Infostealer and Monero Crypto Miner
item.description: Cyber threat actors are infecting victims with the Vidar stealer and the XMRig cryptocurrency miner in a new malicious campaign

item.title: China-Linked UAT-7810 Expands ORB Network With New LONGLEASH Malware
item.description:
A Chinese threat actor tracked as UAT-7810 is actively refining its bespoke malware to expand its Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by breaking into internet-facing networking devices.
According to findings from Cisco Talos, UAT-7810 is an advanced persistent threat (APT) actor that's responsible for maintaining and proliferating LapDogs, an ORB network that first came to light in June 2025.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ESET Threat Report H1 2026
item.description: A view of the H1 2026 threat landscape as seen by ESET telemetry and from the perspective of ESET threat detection and research experts.

item.title: NCSC Touts National Scale, AI-Powered “Cyber Shield” for Defense
item.description: The National Cyber Security Centre wants to work with AI partners to build a new “Cyber Shield” to defend the UK

item.title: State IDs for AI Agents: Will Estonia Set a Precedent?
item.description: The world's digital testing ground plans to help people use AI agents for government purposes.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: 15-Year-Old GhostLock Flaw Enables Root and Container Escape on Most Linux Distros
item.description:
Researchers at&nbsp;Nebula Security&nbsp;have disclosed GhostLock (CVE-2026-43499), a 15-year-old Linux kernel flaw that lets any logged-in user take full&nbsp;root&nbsp;control of a machine that has not been patched.
The vulnerable code has shipped by default in essentially every mainstream distribution since 2011. The flaw needs no special permission, no unusual settings, and no network
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Actively Exploited Adobe, Joomla, and Langflow Flaws to KEV
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added four security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2026-48282 (CVSS score: 10.0) - A path traversal vulnerability in Adobe ColdFusion that could lead to arbitrary code execution in the context of the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Big Brand Jobs Scam Targets Marketing Pros' Google Accounts
item.description: The phishing campaign uses several tactics, including nested redirects, to evade detection and steal credentials from unsuspecting targets.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Dialogflow CX 'Rogue Agent' Flaw Enabled AI Chatbot Data Theft
item.description: Varonis reported the flaw to Google in late 2025 and it has been addressed, but it reminds defenders to take a fresh look at their AI Infrastructure security.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: RedWing MaaS Packages Android Bank Fraud as a Telegram Rental Service
item.description:
A new Android malware operation called RedWing is being rented out on Telegram as a ready-made bank-fraud service. It lets even low-skill criminals take over a victim's phone, steal their banking logins, and capture the one-time codes that protect their accounts.
Zimperium's zLabs, which found the operation, says it looks like a new variant of Oblivion, a $300-a-month rent-a-malware tool
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Rogue Agent Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Hijack Google Dialogflow CX Chatbots
item.description:
A critical flaw in Google's Dialogflow CX could have let an attacker with edit rights on one Code Block-enabled agent compromise other Code Block-enabled agents in the same Google Cloud project.
From there, they could read live conversations, steal the data users shared, and make the bots send attacker-written messages, including requests to re-enter a password.
Security firm Varonis found it
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Suspected Chinese Threat Group Targets Universities via Vulnerable Roundcube Servers
item.description: A suspected Chinese threat cluster is exploiting Roundcube vulnerabilities to compromise university networks in the US and Canada and harvest user credentials

item.title: 'GitLost' Flaw Leaks Private Data From GitHub's Agentic Workflows
item.description: The flaw allows an unauthenticated attacker to craft a GitHub Issue in an org's public repository and then silently pull data from its private repos, too.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: DEBULL Tooling Abuses Microsoft Device-Code Flow to Target M365 Accounts
item.description:
A Microsoft 365 device code phishing campaign has been observed leveraging collaboration-themed lures to take control of victim accounts between the last week of June 2026 and into early July, per findings from ZeroBEC.
"The campaign did not depend on a fake Microsoft password page. It used a malicious collaboration-style lure to push users into the legitimate Microsoft device login experience,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Search Uploads Can Train AI Unless You Opt Out
item.description: <p>Google Search uploads may be used to train AI unless users opt out, raising privacy and data governance concerns for businesses.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-search-uploads-train-ai-opt-out/">Google Search Uploads Can Train AI Unless You Opt Out</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Public GitHub Issue Could Trick GitHub Agentic Workflows Into Leaking Private Repo Data
item.description:
A public issue can trick GitHub Agentic Workflows into leaking the contents of an organization's private repositories, researchers at Noma Security have shown.
The attacker needs only to open a normal-looking issue on a public repository, with no stolen credentials and no access to the organization. If that organization has given the agent read access across its repositories, private ones
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Scattered Spider’s Structure More Like a Cybercrime Collective Than a Unified Gang
item.description: Group-IB analysis argued Scattered Spider is a decentralized collective of independent clusters

item.title: Court Filing Reveals Windows Device ID Helped FBI Trace Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker
item.description:
U.S. prosecutors linked an alleged Scattered Spider hacker to a break-in at a luxury jewelry retailer using a persistent Windows device ID, according to a newly unsealed federal complaint.
Microsoft records tied that ID first to the account the attackers used to keep access during the May 2025 intrusion, then to online accounts prosecutors say belong to 19-year-old Peter Stokes.
Stokes is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Writer AI Flaw Could Let Agent Previews Leak Session Tokens Across Tenants
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched critical session isolation vulnerability in Writer, an enterprise generative artificial intelligence (AI) platform, that could result in cross-tenant compromise.
The one-click vulnerability has been codenamed WriteOut by the Sand Security Research team.
"An outsider could go from having no access to taking over any Writer AI
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What Changes When Your Software Supply Chain Includes AI Writing Your Code?
item.description:
Software supply chain security was hard enough. Then AI joined the build pipeline.
For five years, "software supply chain security" meant one question: what's in your code? Which open-source packages, which versions, which transitive dependencies three layers deep that nobody chose on purpose?
SolarWinds, Log4Shell, and XZ Utils all taught the same lesson: the risk lives less in the code a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Is Suing Chinese Scammers Who Are Using Gemini
item.description: <p>Not sure <a href="https://arstechnica.com/google/2026/06/google-sues-chinese-cybercrime-network-that-used-gemini-to-automate-scams/">this</a> will have any effect, but I support the effort:</p> <blockquote><p>According to Google&#8217;s legal filing, Outsider Enterprise operates through Telegram. The group offers phishing-as-a-service to individuals who may not be technically savvy enough to set up fraudulent websites and text campaigns on their own. In its Telegram channels, Outsider Enterprise reportedly provided instructions on how to use Google&#8217;s Gemini AI to create websites that imitate those of Google, YouTube, and government agencies such as New York&#8217;s E-ZPass. The group offered nearly 300 scam templates...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: UK Government Launches Cyber Resilience Pledge, Claiming 60+ Signatories
item.description: More than 60 organizations, including M&amp;S, Microsoft UK and Vodafone, have signed the UK government's Cyber Resilience Pledge, a new initiative aimed at boosting cyber security and resilience across British businesses

item.title: Suspected China-Aligned Hackers Exploit Roundcube Flaws Against Universities
item.description:
A suspected China-aligned threat activity cluster has been observed exploiting Roundcube webmail software belonging to physics and engineering departments of U.S. and Canadian universities as part of a new campaign.
The activity involves the exploitation of now-patched, critical security flaws in the open-source email solution, such as CVE-2024-42009 (CVSS score: 9.3), to siphon credentials,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Exploit Maximum Severity Adobe ColdFusion Flaw
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting an Adobe ColdFusion vulnerability which has a CVSS score of 10.0

item.title: Phishing Attacks Targeted Facebook Users With Fake Verification Offer
item.description: Attacks also used a compromised chatbot in campaign to steal sensitive information from Business Users

item.title: CERT/CC Warns of Hidden Admin Backdoor in Tenda Router Firmware
item.description:
Several versions of firmware released by Chinese network device manufacturer Tenda have been found to embed an undocumented authentication backdoor that enables administrative access to the devices' web management interfaces, the CERT Coordination Center (CERT/CC) warned Monday.
"An attacker can exploit this vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-11405, to bypass the password verification process
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: BeyondTrust Patches Critical Auth Bypass Flaws in Remote Support and PRA
item.description:
BeyondTrust has released updates to address two critical security flaws affecting Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) products that, if successfully exploited, could allow unauthenticated attackers to take control of susceptible devices.
The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2026-40138 (CVSS score: 9.2) - A pre-authentication vulnerability exists in the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Architecture of an AI-Powered Breach: The Shadow Supply Chain | UpGuard
item.description: Decode three real-world AI breaches. Learn how Shadow AI pipelines bypass security and how real-time browser guidance can stop data leaks.

item.title: Decoding the Copilot Ecosystem | UpGuard
item.description: Don't let Copilot extend its permission sprawl. Learn the structural risks of M365 and GitHub Copilot, and how to enforce real-time browser guardrails.

item.title: Governing Excessive Agency in the Anthropic Ecosystem | UpGuard
item.description: Agentic AI is moving fast. Learn how to govern Claude safely, neutralize Excessive Agency, and enforce real-time browser-level guardrails.

item.title: The 2026 Enterprise AI Security Index | UpGuard
item.description: Cut through the marketing hype. Uncover the architectural risks, data defaults, and vulnerabilities of leading AI models in our AI Security Index.

item.title: Best TPRM Software for Higher Education: What to Look For | UpGuard
item.description: Learn how to evaluate higher education TPRM software, including HECVAT workflows, vendor monitoring, remediation, audit readiness, and reporting.

item.title: Higher Education TPRM in 2026: New Research Maps the Vendor Visibility Gap | UpGuard
item.description: Explore UpGuard’s latest research into higher education third-party risk, including supplier concentration and systemic vendor exposure.

item.title: Best Threat Intelligence Platforms and Vendors | UpGuard
item.description: Compare the best threat intelligence platforms (TIPs) and vendors of 2026. Learn key features, criteria, and find the perfect tool for your security team.

item.title: Best Vulnerability Management Tools & Software in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Discover the best vulnerability management tools on the market and learn which vulnerability detection solution is best for your organization.

item.title: 'BusySnake' Infostealer Slithers Into Critical Infrastructure Networks
item.description: A threat group researchers call &quot;Armored Likho&quot; has gained access to government agencies and electrical power entities in Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: CitrixBleed-ing Again? NetScaler Vulnerability Under Attack
item.description: Attackers wasted little time targeting the latest memory disclosure flaw in Citrix's NetScaler products, after researchers published a proof-of-concept exploit (PoC).
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: DHS Confirms Breach of Homeland Security Information Network
item.description: <p>DHS confirmed a breach of the Homeland Security Information Network, an unclassified platform used for security and emergency coordination.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-dhs-hsin-breach-2026/">DHS Confirms Breach of Homeland Security Information Network</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Use New Cavern C2 Framework to Target Israeli Organizations
item.description:
An Iranian hacking group affiliated with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) has been wielding a previously undocumented modular command-and-control (C2) framework dubbed Cavern (aka Cav3rn) targeting Israeli organizations.
The activity, which has primarily singled out IT providers and government sectors, has been attributed to a threat cluster tracked by Check Point Research
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 16-Year-Old Linux KVM Flaw Lets Guest VMs Escape to Host on Intel and AMD x86 Systems
item.description:
A use-after-free bug in Linux's KVM hypervisor can be triggered from a guest virtual machine to corrupt the shadow-page state of the host kernel that runs it.
Dubbed 'Januscape' and tracked as&nbsp;CVE-2026-53359, the flaw sits in the shadow MMU code that KVM shares across both Intel and AMD. The public proof-of-concept panics the host; the researcher claims that a separate, unreleased exploit
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Attack Exposes Microsoft 365 MFA Gap
item.description: <p>A password spray campaign targeting Azure CLI sign-ins exposed how narrow Conditional Access policies can leave Microsoft 365 accounts vulnerable even when MFA is enabled.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-azure-cli-mfa-gap/">Azure CLI Password Spray Attack Exposes Microsoft 365 MFA Gap</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: JadePuffer: The First Complete LLM-Driven Ransomware Attack
item.description: An &quot;agentic threat actor&quot; successfully exploited a Langflow flaw to steal data from a production database server and encrypt other systems.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Threat Actors Probe Gitea Docker Flaw CVE-2026-20896 13 Days After Disclosure
item.description:
Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently patched critical security flaw in Gitea Docker images, according to Sysdig.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-20896 (CVSS score: 9.8), a vulnerability that stems from the DevOps platform trusting the "X-WEBAUTH-USER" header from any source IP address, effectively allowing an unauthenticated internet client to get elevated
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Iran-Nexus Hacking Group Targets Israel Government and IT Sectors
item.description: Check Point researchers have identified a new cyber adversary targeting Israeli government and IT businesses, tracked as ‘Cavern Manticore’

item.title: Indirect Prompt Injection in Web Content Targets AI Agents
item.description: Zscaler found sites hiding prompt-injection text to manipulate AI agents into crypto payments

item.title: Opera GX Flaw Let Sites Auto-Install Mods to Steal Data
item.description: Opera GX flaw let sites automatically install mods to steal data from other pages, now patched

item.title: Gemini Spark App Connections Arrive Before Enterprise Controls
item.description: <p>Google Gemini Spark’s new app connections move the AI agent into files, apps, and MCP servers, creating new governance questions for IT teams.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-gemini-spark-app-integrations/">Gemini Spark App Connections Arrive Before Enterprise Controls</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Proxy Botnets, Browser Ransomware, AI Agent Tricks, Fake PoC Malware and More
item.description:
A streaming box should not need a threat model. Neither should a username field, a demo repo, a reset flow, or a browser permission prompt. That is the irritating part this week: the risky pieces were ordinary.
Home devices became a routing cover. Clean code pulled dirt from a dependency. Identity shortcuts aged badly. AI systems trusted the wrong instructions. Same soft spot throughout: trust
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How to Evaluate an AI SOC Platform in 2026: 6 Capabilities That Separate Leaders from Bolt-On AI solutions
item.description:
Building a shortlist for an AI SOC evaluation can be tough. SIEM, SOAR, and pureplay AI SOC vendors are all saying the same thing. But behind the identical label sit very different products, from chat assistants bolted onto a legacy SIEM to agent platforms that run detection, triage, investigation, and response on their own data foundation.
Whether a platform will materially change outcomes for
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Suspected China-Nexus Hackers Use Fake Indian Tax Filing Utility to Deploy DcRAT
item.description:
A suspected China-nexus threat activity cluster has been observed targeting Indian taxpayers, tax professionals, and corporate finance teams to deliver a remote access trojan designed to steal sensitive data from compromised hosts.
The multi-stage campaign, codenamed Operation DragonReturn by Seqrite Labs, involves sending spear-phishing emails impersonating the Income Tax Department of India.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
